paper_title: Geochronological and Glaciological Analysis of Antarctic Glaciers

methods.description: This method involves the collection and analysis of erratic samples and bedrock to measure cosmogenic nuclides, which help determine the exposure ages and constrain the ice thickness during the Last Glacial Maximum (LGM).

steps.description: We measured 10 Be and 26 Al in erratics we collected from Diamond Hill, as well as from Bibra, Dubris and Magnis Valleys.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and measure 10 Be and 26 Al in erratic samples

steps.description: We used conventional purification methods including heavy liquid separation, surfactant separation, and dilute HF etching at ~60°C to isolate pure quartz for exposure dating.
steps.searchable_summary: purify quartz using heavy liquid separation and HF etching

steps.description: We analyzed samples for in situ 10 Be at the Center for Accelerator Mass Spectrometry (CAMS) at Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze in situ 10 Be at CAMS

steps.description: We also measured cosmogenic 14 C produced in situ in quartz along an elevation transect of granitic bedrock from Diamond Hill to constrain the LGM ice thickness.
steps.searchable_summary: measure in situ 14 C in quartz to constrain LGM ice thickness

methods.description: This method involves tuning a flowband model to estimate ice-flow parameters by minimizing the mismatch between modeled and observed glacier data.

steps.description: We tune a steady-state version of the flowband model to estimate poorly constrained ice-flow parameter values by minimizing the mismatch between the modeled and observed modern glacier surface elevation and the modern surface velocity at each model grid point.
steps.searchable_summary: tune flowband model to match observed glacier data

steps.description: We vary the basal sliding factor (fs) and the ice-flow deformation factor (fd) defined at each model grid point within a plausible range of values.
steps.searchable_summary: adjust basal sliding and deformation factors in model
inputs: plausible range of fs and fd

methods.description: This method evaluates the model output by comparing it with geochronology data to estimate glacier centerline elevations during the LGM.

steps.description: We can determine the possible range of glacier centerline elevations at the LGM by calculating a linear best-fit curve to the LGM deposits in the valley floor and on the valley walls.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate glacier centerline elevations using linear fit

steps.description: Model results are compared against these projected elevations in Figure 10, Figure 11, and Figure 12.
steps.searchable_summary: compare model results with projected glacier elevations
